title: DORA Art.8 — ICT risk identification: newly discovered high-severity exposure on critical financial services
exclusions: Does NOT replace the scanner; consumes the scanner's output and prioritises findings on the subset of hosts classified as 'critical financial service' per DORA Art.8(1).
evidence: Saved search 'dora_art8_new_crit_exposure' (every 15m) emits, per finding: finding_id, ict_service, cvss, cve, discovered_time, registration_sla_met (boolean), to the compliance_summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.8 requires continuous identification of ICT-related risks; a 15-minute detection + 24h register SLA directly maps to that obligation for critical services.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.8 (Identification) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.43: DORA Art.8 — ICT risk identification: newly discovered high-severity exposure on critical financial services.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.43' running on sourcetype tenable:sc:vuln and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Creating a test finding with CVSS=8 on a host tagged criticality_tier=critical causes the UC to fire within 15m naming the CVE and ict_service.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A CVSS=5 or non-critical host finding does NOT fire.

dataSources: `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln OR sourcetype=qualys:host; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_ict_service; DORA critical-service lookup `dora_critical_services.csv`.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for Tenable, Splunk Add-on for Qualys
spl:
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) cvss>=7 earliest=-15m
| rename dest AS hostname
| lookup dora_critical_services.csv hostname OUTPUT ict_service criticality_tier
| where criticality_tier="critical"
| eval registration_sla_met=if(relative_time(now(),"-24h")<=_time,1,0)
| stats count BY ict_service cvss cve hostname registration_sla_met
description: Identifies newly-discovered CVSS>=7 exposures on hosts supporting a DORA-critical financial service. Measures whether the finding is registered in the ICT risk register within the 24h target implied by Art.8 'continuous identification'.
value: Produces per-finding evidence that critical-service exposure is identified and logged within the DORA-aligned window, rather than relying on monthly scanner review meetings.
implementation: (1) Onboard scanner output; (2) tag hosts with ict_service + criticality_tier nightly; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) each hit opens a risk-register entry (ServiceNow) within 24h; (5) weekly report to ICT risk committee; (6) breach of 24h SLA escalates to the Head of IR and Board/Audit Committee.
visualization: Timechart of new criticals per ICT service, table of unresolved findings, single value 'findings past 24h SLA'.
knownFalsePositives: Scanner re-baselines after signature updates can produce a one-time spike of 're-discovered' findings — cross-reference against first_seen and exclude.
grandmaExplanation: Produces -finding evidence that critical-service exposure is identified and logged within the DORA-aligned window, rather than relying on monthly scanner review meetings — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for Tenable, Splunk Add-on for Qualys.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln OR sourcetype=qualys:host; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_ict_service; DORA critical-service lookup `dora_critical_services.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Onboard scanner output; (2) tag hosts with ict_service + criticality_tier nightly; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) each hit opens a risk-register entry (ServiceNow) within 24h; (5) weekly report to ICT risk committee; (6) breach of 24h SLA escalates to the Head of IR and Board/Audit Committee.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) cvss>=7 earliest=-15m
| rename dest AS hostname
| lookup dora_critical_services.csv hostname OUTPUT ict_service criticality_tier
| where criticality_tier="critical"
| eval registration_sla_met=if(relative_time(now(),"-24h")<=_time,1,0)
| stats count BY ict_service cvss cve hostname registration_sla_met
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Art.8 — ICT risk identification: newly discovered high-severity exposure on critical financial services** — Produces per-finding evidence that critical-service exposure is identified and logged within the DORA-aligned window, rather than relying on monthly scanner review meetings.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln OR sourcetype=qualys:host; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_ict_service; DORA critical-service lookup `dora_critical_services.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for Tenable, Splunk Add-on for Qualys. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where criticality_tier="critical"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **registration_sla_met** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ict_service cvss cve hostname registration_sla_met** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timechart of new criticals per ICT service, table of unresolved findings, single value 'findings past 24h SLA'.
